search_queries_used: site:eaaci.org international urticaria guideline 2022 definition classification diagnosis management
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic urticaria subtype or trigger conclusion
automatic antihistamine, biologic, dose, investigation, referral, or emergency instruction
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The accessible exact source supports limited definition and impact fields only.
All diagnosis and management remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children with clinician-established or suspected urticaria returning for review; angioedema, airway, anaphylaxis, pregnancy, pediatric, inducible, medicine, and chronic-disease context require qualified assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, allergy, dermatology, pediatric, and emergency-linked assessment.
UAE_applicability: Definition and impact are transferable, but UAE allergy, emergency, medicine, formulary, laboratory, and referral pathways remain local.
recency_verification: The official EAACI 2022 guideline page was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No newer published international EAACI urticaria guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact guideline abstract supports documenting an urticaria follow-up, interval course, wheals, angioedema or both, duration and clinician skin or mucosal assessment without inferring acute versus chronic subtype or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact impact statement supports documenting severity, function, quality-of-life, work or school impact and patient concerns without calculating a score.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact wheal timing, angioedema, airway and anaphylaxis assessment, subtype, triggers, medicines, inducibility, disease activity score, diagnosis, tests, treatment, dose, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
